urgency.message: 1 critical CVE(s) and 7 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges
items.summary:
Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko admitted to working on the development of a loader for the Conti gang.
The post Ukrainian Man Pleads Guilty in US to Conti Ransomware Charges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems
items.summary:
The pharmaceutical giant says the attackers gained access to personal data stored on the compromised systems. 
The post Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Says Hackers Breached IT Systems appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker
items.summary:
French officials say roughly 73,000 government accounts were affected, while the threat actor claims to have stolen messages and user data from the sovereign Tchap platform.
The post French Government Messaging Platform Breached by Mysterious ‘Misere’ Hacker appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: 152 Chrome Wallpaper Extensions with 105K Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Traffic
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a network of 152 Google Chrome extensions that act as new tab live wallpaper add-ons to distribute a potentially unwanted program (PUP) family.

The cluster spans 38 separate Chrome Web Store publisher accounts and three brand backends: tabplugins[.]com, yow

items.title: ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack
items.summary:
The extortion group threatens to leak 297 GB of data allegedly stolen from the Council of Europe, including employee personal information.
The post ShinyHunters Claims Council of Europe Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Cybersecurity Experts Urge US to Lift Ban on Anthropic's Frontier AI Models
items.summary: Access to two Anthropic large language models, Mythos 5 and Fable 5, has effectively been banned to any non-US nationals by the Trump administration

items.title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
items.summary:
An attacker tampered with trusted JavaScript files used by WordPress sites running PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning those files into a way to break into the sites.

When a site administrator was logged in as the file loaded, the code created an admin account under the attacker's con

items.title: FBI, Google Dismantle ‘Outsider Enterprise’ Phishing Service
items.summary:
The platform used more than 9,000 phishing sites, stealing nearly 4 million credit cards and causing roughly $1.9 billion in losses.
The post FBI, Google Dismantle ‘Outsider Enterprise’ Phishing Service appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UK Government Finds 400+ Vulnerabilities in AI Hackathons
items.summary: Government departments find hundreds of vulnerabilities after testing frontier models

items.title: Meta Tapped a Pentagon Supplier to Prototype Face Recognition for Its Glasses
items.summary: Rank One, whose board includes a former CIA deputy director and a former FBI science chief, supplied face recognition to Meta for internal development of its smart glasses app.

items.title: Maine Takes Breach Reporting Portal Offline After Fake Entries
items.summary: The Office of the Maine Attorney General has suspended its breach reporting portal

items.summary: Information published.

items.title: CVE-2026-49762 Unbounded integer parsing in the Version module enables CPU and memory exhaustion denial of service
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: CVE-2026-7774 tarfile.data_filter path traversal bypass allows writing outside the extraction directory
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: CVE-2026-6253 proxy credentials leak over redirect-to proxy
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: CVE-2026-5545 wrong reuse of HTTP Negotiate connection
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: CVE-2026-6429 netrc credential leak with reused proxy connection
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: Maine Disables Data Breach Portal Due to Fake Submissions
items.summary:
Someone posted fake VRChat and Discord data breach reports on the system, prompting the Maine AG to take action.
The post Maine Disables Data Breach Portal Due to Fake Submissions  appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CVE-2026-45445 AES-OCB IV Ignored on EVP_Cipher() Path
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: CVE-2026-42768 Multi-RecipientInfo Bleichenbacher Oracle in CMS_decrypt() and PKCS7_decrypt()
items.summary: Information published.

items.title: Evil MSI Background: BASE64 Statistical Analysis, (Mon, Jun 15th)
items.summary: I like it when a fellow handler posts a diary entry about images with malicious content. Last one is Xavier: "The Evil MSI Background is Back!".

items.title: Sniper Dz Scams Target MENA Users via Fake Facebook Offers and Browser Alerts
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of fraudulent activity targeting users across the Middle East and North Africa by employing various fraudulent Facebook accounts impersonating politicians, public figures, and trusted organizations.

"These accounts promoted fake offers, including fre

items.title: Palo Alto Warns of Active Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Flaw
items.summary:
Palo Alto Networks has revealed that it has observed "active exploitation" of a recently disclosed PAN-OS vulnerability by an unknown threat actor to obtain unauthorized access to GlobalProtect portals.

The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-0257 (CVSS score: 7.8), an authentication bypass flaw

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 15th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9972, (Mon, Jun 15th)
Belarus-linked hackers target Gmail accounts of Polish public figures and their families
items.summary: Poland has warned that Ghostwriter, the Belarus-linked hacker group, has expanded its phishing operations to target personal Gmail accounts belonging to senior public figures and their relatives.

items.title: FBI disrupts massive AI-powered phishing service using a million URLs
items.summary: In a coordinated effort, the FBI, working with Google and Black Lotus Labs, has dismantled a massive Chinese phishing-as-a-service operation called Outsider Enterprise with thousands of phishing websites used to steal credit card data and passwords. [...]

items.title: Ex-school district employee jailed for hacks on former employer
items.summary: A former  IT employee at an Iowa school district was sentenced to 21 months in prison after conducting a prolonged cyberattack against the former employer that disrupted classroom operations, deleted accounts, and caused tens of thousands of dollars in damages. [...]

items.title: NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks
items.summary:
By default, npm install will no longer execute scripts from dependencies, unless explicitly allowed.
The post NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
items.summary: Chinese hackers took control of a target organization's authentication stack and maintained persistence for 10 years, with full visibility into the administrative activity. [...]

items.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
items.summary:
Splunk has released security updates to address a critical security flaw in Splunk Enterprise that could be exploited to conduct unauthenticated file operations and even remote code execution.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, is rated 9.8 on the CVSS scoring system.

"In Splunk Enterpr

items.title: The FCC Wants to Kill Burner Phones
items.summary: Plus: AI bug hunting fuels Microsoft’s biggest-ever Patch Tuesday, ShinyHunters ransomware gang exploits an Oracle zero-day, and more.

items.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
items.summary: The US government has ordered Anthropic to block all foreign nationals from accessing Fable 5 and Mythos 5, forcing the company to suspend both models worldwide. Anthropic is complying but disputes the basis, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the capability widely available elsewhere. [...]

items.title: Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls
items.summary:
Anthropic takes Fable 5 and Mythos 5 offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent use by foreign nationals.
The post Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
items.summary: Anthropic said on Friday it will "abruptly disable" its most advanced artificial intelligence (AI) models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access to the models for foreign nationals, whether inside or outside the U.S., citing national securi

items.title: Tracing Digital Intent: New MacOS Tahoe 26 Artifact Discovered
items.summary:
Unit 42 has discovered a new macOS Tahoe 26 forensic artifact that tracks user menu selections across the operating system. Learn more here. 
The post Tracing Digital Intent: New MacOS Tahoe 26 Artifact Discovered appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
items.summary: A major bug in Oracle's ERP software disproportionately affected American universities, and hackers have capitalized by stealing gobs of data.

items.title: Maine disables data breach notification portal after fake disclosures
items.summary: Maine has taken its public data breach reporting portal offline after fraudulent breach disclosures were published on the state's website, prompting a review of procedures to prevent abuse in the future. [...]

items.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
items.summary:
Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them.

The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF r

items.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
items.summary:
Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting Americans.

The network is said to be behind the development and management of a phishing-as-a-service (P

items.title: phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade
items.summary: A 10-year-old authentication bypass vulnerability discovered in the phpBB forum software allows an attacker to log in as any user, including administrators. [...]

items.title: Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation
items.summary: A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland to the United States last year has pleaded guilty to conspiracy charges tied to the Conti ransomware operation. [...]

items.title: Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
items.summary: About 7 million customers of the genetics testing company had their data stolen by hackers starting in April 2023, and many had their information posted on the dark web.

items.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock
items.summary: It is the first lapse of the spy program, known as Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA), since it was passed into law in 2008.

items.title: Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
items.summary: More than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) are distributing a Linux rootkit and infostealer malware targeting credentials and access tokens. [...]

items.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
items.summary: The penalty is the largest ever issued by the commission for a personal data breach, surpassing the record 134.8 billion won ($88.8 million) fine levied against SK Telecom earlier this year.

items.title: Ransomware Payment Crypto Laundering Platform Taken Out by FBI and Europol
items.summary: Domain of dark web money laundering platform AudiA6 seized and suspects arrested in joint operation by the FBI, Europol and others

items.title: Claude Fable 5 Doesn't Change the Mythos Security Story
items.summary: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explained.

items.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly in Cybersecurity – Week 24
items.summary: Authorities seize crypto-laundering network and fake recruitment sites, JDY botnet targets U.S. military, and Miasma worm infects Microsoft and PyPi repos.

items.title: GitHub to Update npm to Thwart Software Supply Chain Attacks
items.summary: NPM, part of GitHub, announced a new version of the npm package manager with several security improvements, including disabling install scripts
